vocabulary.definition: a stick used to fly on in the wizarding world
vocabulary.example: It contains your new Nimbus Two Thousand, but I don’t want everybody knowing you’ve got a broomstick or they’ll all want one.
But as all they knew for sure about the mysterious object was that it was about two inches long, they didn’t have much chance of guessing what it was without further clues.
“A Nimbus Two Thousand!” Ron moaned enviously. “I’ve never even touched one.”

vocabulary.definition: to dive or move quickly through the air
vocabulary.example: What a feeling—he swooped in and out of the goal posts and then sped up and down the field.
vocabulary.pageHint: first time on the Quidditch field

vocabulary.definition: the time when day turns into evening
vocabulary.example: As seven o’clock drew nearer, Harry left the castle and set off in the dusk toward the Quidditch field.

vocabulary.definition: a person who watches an event
vocabulary.example: Hundreds of seats were raised in stands around the field so that the spectators were high enough to see what was going on.

vocabulary.definition: a jet-black Quidditch ball that tries to knock players off their brooms
to flap wings or move lightly and quickly
vocabulary.example: They had hardly fluttered out of the way when another owl dropped a letter on top of the parcel.

vocabulary.definition: a large orange vegetable often used in fall cooking
vocabulary.example: On Halloween morning they woke to the delicious smell of baking pumpkin wafting through the corridors.

vocabulary.definition: a huge, stupid, dangerous magical creature
vocabulary.example: Troll—in the dungeons—thought you ought to know.

vocabulary.definition: so frightened that you cannot move
vocabulary.example: a high, petrified scream—and it was coming from the chamber they’d just chained up.

vocabulary.definition: wild and out of control with anger
vocabulary.example: The shouting and the echoes seemed to be driving the troll berserk.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Receives a Nimbus Two Thousand and trains for Quidditch before facing a mountain troll.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Sticks by Harry through broomstick trouble, charms class, and the troll fight.

characters.name: Hermione Granger
characters.description: Still angry at the boys until the troll attack changes everything.

characters.name: Oliver Wood
characters.description: Gryffindor Quidditch captain who teaches Harry the rules of the game.

background.hook: Harry gets an amazing new broomstick and his first Quidditch lesson, but Halloween night turns terrifying when a mountain troll breaks into the castle — and Hermione is trapped alone with it.
background.readingFocus: Watch how Harry earns the broomstick, what happens in Charms class with Wingardium Leviosa, and how the three first years work together against the troll.

options.text: a stick wizards use to fly

miniCheck.stem: In this chapter, a troll is —
If you are petrified, you are —

options.text: so scared you can barely move

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry gets a racing broomstick and Quidditch training, then he and Ron fight a troll to save Hermione
Harry visits Diagon Alley for the first time
The Sorting Hat places students in their houses
Harry finds the Mirror of Erised

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers the Nimbus Two Thousand, Harry's first Quidditch lesson, the Halloween troll alert, and the fight that makes Hermione their friend.
Professor McGonagall's letter says the parcel contains Harry's new Nimbus Two Thousand.

comprehension.stem: Which spell makes the feather float in Charms class?
comprehension.explanation: Hermione succeeds with Wingardium Leviosa, and Ron later uses the same spell on the troll's club.

comprehension.stem: What does Professor Quirrell announce at the Halloween feast?

options.text: A troll is in the dungeons

comprehension.explanation: Quirrell gasps that there is a troll in the dungeons before fainting.

comprehension.stem: Why is Hermione crying in the corridor after Charms?

options.text: She overheard Ron say no one can stand her

comprehension.explanation: Ron tells Harry she is a nightmare, and Harry sees Hermione in tears right after she passes them.

comprehension.stem: Why do Harry and Ron leave the line of students going to the dormitories?

options.text: They realize Hermione does not know about the troll
Percy orders them to explore the dungeons
They want to find the three-headed dog
Snape sends them on a detention

comprehension.explanation: Harry grabs Ron's arm and says Hermione does not know about the troll.

comprehension.stem: How do Harry and Ron finally knock out the troll?

options.text: Harry sticks his wand up its nose and Ron levitates its club onto its head
They lock it in a classroom and leave
Professor McGonagall stuns it from the doorway

comprehension.explanation: Harry's wand goes up the troll's nostril, and Ron's Wingardium Leviosa drops the club on the troll's head.

options.text: the time when evening is beginning

vocabulary.explanation: Harry walks to the Quidditch field at seven o'clock in the dusk.

vocabulary.stem: On Halloween morning they woke to the delicious smell of baking ___ wafting through the corridors.
vocabulary.explanation: Pumpkin baking fills the corridors on Halloween morning.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'mysterious'?
vocabulary.explanation: Harry and Ron know little about the mysterious object guarded under the trapdoor.

vocabulary.stem: What a feeling—he ___ in and out of the goal posts and then sped up and down the field.
vocabulary.explanation: Harry swoops through the goal posts on his new Nimbus.

options.text: watches an event from the stands

vocabulary.explanation: Raised seats let spectators see the Quidditch field clearly.

cloze.title: Halloween
paragraphs: Harry receives a new (1) ___ in the mail and trains with Oliver Wood at (2) ___ on the Quidditch field. Wood shows him the Quaffle, the (3) ___, and the Golden Snitch.
On Halloween, Charms class practices Wingardium Leviosa. After Ron insults Hermione, a (4) ___ breaks into the castle during the feast. Harry and Ron hear a (5) ___ scream from the girls' bathroom.

Harry jams his wand up the troll's nose while Ron uses Wingardium Leviosa to drop the club on its head. The troll goes (6) ___ before collapsing. Hermione lies to protect them, and from that night on she becomes their (7) ___.

gaps.explanation: McGonagall's parcel contains Harry's new Nimbus Two Thousand broomstick.
Harry heads to the field at seven o'clock in the dusk.
Wood introduces the two jet-black Bludgers.
Quirrell announces a troll in the dungeons at the feast.
A high, petrified scream comes from the bathroom they locked.
The shouting drives the troll berserk before Ron's spell works.
After the troll, Hermione Granger becomes their friend.
